steps.title: Identify what the text is doing
steps.content:
The passage opens by stating the researchers' conclusion: that people are more likely to behave politely when listening to ideas they disagree with if they think about values before they engage in a discussion.

Then it explains the experimental method—assigning participants to a group that wrote about values and a control group that wrote about a drink—and reports the results showing greater civility in the values group.

steps.title: Match the purpose to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly names this purpose: explaining a study's conclusion and how the research team arrived at it.

Choices A, B, and
  • C mischaracterize the text—A invents a pre-existing belief rather than presenting the study's own finding
  • B adds unsupported surprise
  • C suggests methodological critique when the text simply describes the design
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
